info.title: Products
info.x-hubspot-api-use-case: To prepare for an upcoming product launch, use the products API to create a set of products to represent your new offerings. Sales reps can then use these products when creating payment links, invoices, and more.
info.x-hubspot-introduction: Use the products API to create and manage your product library, which is the collection of goods and services that your company offers. Products in your product library can be used as line items when creating deals, payment links, invoices, and quotes.

post.summary: Read products by ID or property

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Returns a batch of products matching the provided internal IDs or unique property values, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.

get.description: Read an Object identified by `{productId}`. `{productId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, or optionally any unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param.  Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: productId
parameters.description: The unique internal identifier of the product to retrieve.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object

delete.description: Move an Object identified by `{productId}` to the recycling bin.

parameters.name: productId
parameters.description: The unique internal identifier of the product to archive.

patch.description: Perform a partial update of an Object identified by `{productId}`. `{productId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, or optionally any unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. Provided property values will be overwritten. Read-only and non-existent properties will result in an error. Properties values can be cleared by passing an empty string.

parameters.name: productId
parameters.description: The unique internal identifier of the product to update.

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object

post.summary: Archive a batch of products by ID
post.description: Returns no content upon successfully archiving the specified batch of products.
Returns the newly created batch of products, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.
Returns the updated batch of products matched by internal ID or unique property values, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.

get.description: Read a page of products. Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to display per page

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The paging cursor token of the last successfully read resource will be returned as the `paging.next.after` JSON property of a paged response containing more results

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored. Usage of this parameter will reduce the maximum number of objects that can be read by a single request

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Create a product with the given properties and return a copy of the object, including the ID. Documentation and examples for creating standard products is provided.
Create or update records identified by a unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. `idProperty` query param refers to a property whose values are unique for the object.
Returns a list of products matching the specified search criteria and filters.
